title: fix: skip redundant KV writes for API key verify tracking
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Silencing the KV chatter
gist.blurb: Our API keys were getting a little too chatty with the database at 5 million requests a day. We've optimized the caching layer to keep things fast, quiet, and highly scalable.
gist.summary: Optimized API key verification to prevent redundant KV writes and eliminate rate-limiting errors at high request volumes.
gist.impact: Backend infrastructure is now more stable and efficient, easily handling millions of daily API requests without hitting database write limits.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot organizing a fast-moving stream of data, preventing the Nomnom creature from being overwhelmed by too many API requests. Represents caching and reducing redundant database writes.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot organizing a neat, single-file line of glowing API keys, while the round tan Nomnom creature happily eats a single data packet instead of a massive pile. A retro server rack in the background glows with a soft lime green (#ecf874) light. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
